text.title: ruby-activerecord Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ruby-activerecord. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Query Objects must accept a relation in the constructor (default Model.all), chain methods that return self, and expose a terminal results method returning the relation — never an array.
rule.rationale: Returning self allows callers to compose filters. Returning the relation instead of to_a defers SQL execution and allows further scoping at the controller level.

rule.statement: Cap Query Objects at approximately 10 chainable methods; if more are needed, split by use case into multiple query objects.
rule.rationale: A 30-method query object that covers every one-off filter becomes a god class — harder to read than inline where chains and impossible to test in isolation.

rule.statement: Eager-load at the controller boundary, never in views. A view triggering a query is invisible in code review.
rule.rationale: View-triggered queries are rendered as template output, not as SQL log entries in the request cycle that code reviewers check. Eager loading in controllers makes query cost reviewable.

rule.statement: Avoid default_scope; use named scopes (scope :active) and force callers to opt in.
rule.rationale: A default_scope { where(deleted_at: nil) } is invisible at every call site. Agents debugging "missing data" rarely think to call withoutGlobalScopes — it costs an hour per incident.

rule.statement: Use find_each / in_batches for any loop over 1k+ records; never User.all.each.
rule.rationale: User.all.each loads the entire result set into memory. find_each streams in batches of 1000, keeping memory constant.
